steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says genetic research "only recently ______ its ancestor to be teosinte grass." The word must fit the idea that research uncovered or determined something previously unknown — specifically the identity of maize's wild ancestor.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — expanded: "Expanded its ancestor to be" is grammatically awkward and semantically wrong. Research can expand knowledge, but it cannot expand an ancestor to be something. ✗
  • Choice B — revealed: "Revealed its ancestor to be teosinte grass" means research uncovered or disclosed what was previously unknown — exactly right. The word "only recently" reinforces that this was a discovery, and "revealed" captures uncovering hidden information. ✓
  • Choice C — encouraged: "Encouraged its ancestor to be" is nonsensical in context. Research cannot encourage a grass to become an ancestor. ✗
  • Choice D — petitioned: "Petitioned its ancestor to be" makes no sense. Research does not petition; this word applies to people making formal requests, not scientific findings. ✗
That matches Choice B — revealed.
